meaning: adj.僧侣的,修道士的,禁欲的
examples: In the study, so choose the most taboo choose a quiet or monkish sorts of good time to word memory.
Though he was never a monk, there was a monkish look about him: the balding head, the frank, kindly eyes, the life spent in rigorous discipline among ancient texts.
Tall and bald with a hearty laugh, the retired interior designer is neither monkish nor zealous.
At Middle Temple Hall, where law students often dine, duck into Fountain Court with its monkish atmosphere.
The gesture takes on more meaning as the monkish Benedict steps down.
